    Zimbabwe Human Rights NGO Forum v Republic of Zimbabwe (SADC (T) 05/2008) [2009] SADCT 2 (1 January 2009)

IN
THE SOUTHERN AFRICAN DEVELOPMENT COMMUNITY (SADC) TRIBUNAL WINDHOEK,
NAMIBIA

CASE
NO SADC (T) 05/2008

IN
THE MATTER BETWEEN

ZIMBABWE
HUMAN RIGHTS NGO FORUM		APPLICANT

And

THE
REPUBLIC OF ZIMBABWE			       RESPONDENT

CORAM:

H.E.
JUSTICE A. G. PILLAY				PRESIDENT

H.E.
JUSTICE I. J. MTAMBO, SC				MEMBER

H.E.JUSTICE
DR. L. A. MONDLANE			MEMBER

H.E
JUSTICE DR. R. KAMBOVO				MEMBER

H.E.
JUSTICE DR. O. B. TSHOSA				MEMBER

APPLICANT芒聙聶S
AGENTS		J.J GAUNTLETT, SC

						F.B.
PELSER Counsel

RESPONDENT芒聙聶S
AGENTS:  	P. MACHAYA, 

DEPUTY ATTORNEY-GENERAL

N. MUTSONZIWA 

CHIEF LAW OFFICER

HON.
JUSTICE M. C. C. MKANDAWIRE	REGISTRAR

MR.
DENNIS SHIVANGULULA			COURT CLERK

____________________________________________________________________

RULING

_____________________________________________________________________

Delivered by H.E.
Justice A.G. Pillay

We have listened to the arguments of learned Counsel on
both sides and are of the considered view, as rightly pointed out by
learned
Counsel for the respondent, that the applicant is not the
proper party in the present case but is in fact the agent or
representative,
pursuant to Rule 24(3) of the Rules of Procedure of
SADC Tribunal (the Rules), of the following persons, namely,

(1)	Barry
T Gondo

(2)	Gabriel
Kawara

(3)	Joyce
Mwachinduka

(4)	Kerina
Gweshe

(5)	Lameck
Sengure

(6)	Nyaradzai
Katsande

(7)	Peter
Chirinda

(8)	Phanuel
Mapingure

(9)	Ruth
Manika

(10)	Sophia
Matasva

(11)	Trust
Shumba

(12)	Mercy
Mangunj

Rule 24(3) of the Rules, in substance, provides for
parties to be represented by agents or other duly authorized persons.

It is quite clear, therefore, that the twelve persons
mentioned above are the aggrieved parties since they complain of
various acts
of violence allegedly committed against them, and
against a spouse of one of them resulting in his death. These acts
would constitute
the succinct statement of the facts in terms of Rule
33(1) (c) of the Rules.

Given that the present application has been made by the
wrong person, we order, in the interests of justice, that the
application
be amended so as to ensure that the proper parties are
the applicants before the Tribunal. 

Delivered in open court this 芒聙娄芒聙娄芒聙娄.Day
of芒聙娄芒聙娄芒聙娄芒聙娄芒聙娄..., at Windhoek in the Republic of Namibia.
